ddls:
CREATE TABLE "storms" (
  "storm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storm */,
  "track_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the track data for the storm */,
  "storm_type" TEXT /* Type of storm (e.g., tornado, hurricane, derecho) */,
  "intensity" TEXT /* Intensity of the storm (e.g., EF1, Category 3) */,
  "det_score" REAL /* Detection score for the storm based on radar data */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the storm */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the storm */,
  "storm_size" REAL /* Size of the storm in square kilometers */,
  "storm_velocity" REAL /* Velocity of the storm in kilometers per hour */,
  "storm_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the storm's movement */,
  "impact_prediction" TEXT /* Prediction of the potential impact on populated areas */,
  "radar_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the radar station that detected the storm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("storm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_storms_track_id FOREIGN KEY ("track_id") REFERENCES tracks ("track_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_storms_storm_type FOREIGN KEY ("storm_type") REFERENCES storm_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_storms_radar_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("radar_station_id") REFERENCES radar_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tracks" (
  "track_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each track */,
  "storm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the storm the track belongs to */,
  "filename" TEXT /* Filename of the radar data */,
  "from_sunrise" INTEGER /* Time from sunrise when the data was collected */,
  "x" REAL /* X-coordinate of the storm */,
  "y" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the storm */,
  "r" REAL /* Radius of the storm */,
  "lon" REAL /* Longitude of the storm */,
  "lat" REAL /* Latitude of the storm */,
  "radius" REAL /* Radius of the storm in km */,
  "local_time" TEXT /* Local time when the data was collected */,
  "velocity_x" REAL /* Velocity of the storm in the x-direction */,
  "velocity_y" REAL /* Velocity of the storm in the y-direction */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the storm */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure at the storm's location */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the storm's location */,
  "radar_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the radar station that collected the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("track_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tracks_radar_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("radar_station_id") REFERENCES radar_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tracks_storm_id FOREIGN KEY ("storm_id") REFERENCES storms ("storm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "radar_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the radar station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the radar station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the radar station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the radar station */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance of the radar station */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance of the radar station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "storm_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storm type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the storm type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the storm type */,
  "average_intensity" TEXT /* Average intensity for storms of this type */,
  "average_duration" REAL /* Average duration of storms of this type in hours */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of occurrence of this storm type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, researcher, emergency management) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for emergency contact */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active (0 or 1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
